title: DocMed Landing Page (app/page.tsx)
content:
## DocMed Landing Page (`app/page.tsx`)

This file defines the main landing page for the DocMed application. It's a client-side component that utilizes Next.js features, framer-motion for animations, and various UI components from `@/components/ui`.

### Purpose

The primary purpose of this file is to render the user-facing landing page of DocMed. It showcases the product's value proposition, features, how it works, and provides clear calls to action for users to sign up or learn more.

### Key Components and Features

-   **`Navbar`**: A fixed navigation bar with branding, navigation links, and authentication buttons. It includes a responsive mobile menu.
-   **`Hero`**: The main hero section featuring a prominent headline, subtext, and calls to action, enhanced by a `DotGlobeHero` component and animations.
-   **`TrustBar`**: A section displaying key attributes and technologies used in DocMed's workflow.
-   **`Features`**: Details the core features of DocMed, each with an icon, title, description, and a highlight.
-   **`HowItWorks`**: Explains the three-step process of using DocMed, from connecting repositories to having self-healing documentation.
-   **`Stats`**: Presents key metrics and statistics related to DocMed's impact.
-   **`CTA` (Call to Action)**: A prominent section encouraging users to sign up, featuring an email input and a sign-up button.
-   **`Footer`**: Contains branding, descriptive text, navigation links, and copyright information.

### Animation and UI Libraries

-   **`framer-motion`**: Used extensively for animating elements like fade-ins, staggering effects, and hover effects.
-   **`lucide-react`**: Provides a set of scalable vector icons used throughout the page.
-   **`@/components/ui/globe-hero`**: A custom component likely responsible for the animated globe effect in the hero section.

### Usage Example

This `app/page.tsx` file is the entry point for the root route (`/`) of the Next.js application. When a user navigates to the root URL, this component is rendered, displaying the entire landing page experience.

```tsx
// app/page.tsx

// ... imports and component definitions ...

export default function Home() {
  return (
    <main>
      <Navbar />
      <Hero />
      <TrustBar />
      <Features />
      <HowItWorks />
      <Stats />
      <CTA />
      <Footer />
    </main>
  );
}
```
